Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null},{"id":16917452,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNjkxNzQ1Mg==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/component:%20numpy.f2py","name":"component: numpy.f2py","color":"009800","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-04-26T17:54:46Z","updated_at":"2024-10-07T00:51:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

I have an old fortran program that I access from python using f2py. I have used this successfully on both linux and windows. I have moved successfully to 1.26 on linux but get a link error (1120 unresolved externals) on windows 11. I replicated the problem using a basic fibonacci fortran program (below)

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
subroutine fib(a, n)
  use iso_c_binding
   integer(c_int), intent(in) :: n
   integer(c_int), intent(out) :: a(n)
   do i = 1, n
      if (i .eq. 1) then
         a(i) = 0.0d0
      elseif (i .eq. 2) then
         a(i) = 1.0d0
      else
         a(i) = a(i - 1) + a(i - 2)
      end if
   end do
end
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/18306","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/18306/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/18306/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/18306/events","html_url":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/18306","id":1669515254,"node_id":"I_kwDOABZIoc5jgsf2","number":18306,"title":"ENH: Add a method to get the Mean Confidence Interval and Prediction Interval for an alpha value. ","user":{"login":"karrablaster123","id":15704074,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE1NzA0MDc0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/15704074?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123","html_url":"https://github.com/karrablaster123","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/karrablaster123/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":37340722,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzNzM0MDcyMg==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/labels/scipy.stats","name":"scipy.stats","color":"0366d6","default":false,"description":""},{"id":37340735,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzNzM0MDczNQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/labels/enhancement","name":"enhancement","color":"bfe5bf","default":true,"description":"A new feature or improvement"},{"id":199201380,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxOTkyMDEzODA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/labels/needs-decision","name":"needs-decision","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"Items that need further discussion before they are merged or closed"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2023-04-15T17:59:34Z","updated_at":"2024-05-22T18:52:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Currently, the linregress function only outputs the following:

- slope
- intercept
- rvalue
- pvalue
- stderr
- intercept_stderr

While it is easy to obtain the intercept and slope confidence intervals from this information, it is very difficult to construct the Confidence Interval for the Mean Response or the Prediction Interval for a New Response as it requires the mean square error and sum of squared deviations from the mean value of x which are private to the function.

### Describe the solution you'd like.

The easiest solution would be to output Sxx and MSE from the linregress function and let the user evaluate the intervals. 

Alternative 1: linregress could accept a flag to evaluate these intervals for a specified confidence level and output the corresponding arrays. The last part is rather inconsistent with the current functionality of the function since it doesn't output y_pred arrays either.  

Alternative 2: Other function(s) to calculate these given x values and the Linear Regression Results tuple. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered.

The OLS regression available from the statsmodels module outputs all the necessary information. 

### Additional context (e.g. screenshots, GIFs)

https://online.stat.psu.edu/stat462/node/126/ - Mean Reponse CI
https://online.stat.psu.edu/stat462/node/127/ - New Response PI

I have implemented this in python for a project I undertook but I am not sure how to contribute to this codebase and whether this is a necessary feature or not, particularly since I'm new to this whole thing of open-source contributions. Kapteyon's kmpfit has some functionality and there are packages that implement bootstrap methods (like seaborn), but there isn't anything that is easy to implement and flexible.  I would love to see a method in the scipy library similar to what is done [in this repo](https://github.com/gjpelletier/delta_method/blob/main/delta_method.py), which already is written to be friendly with scipy.optimize.curve_fit.  ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/comments/2125532036/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
